title: Packages, databases, and web interaction
summary: Work with Python's wider ecosystem by installing packages, storing structured data, and making controlled requests to external systems.

items.prompt: What is `pip` used for in a Python workflow?

solution.summary: It installs and manages external Python packages so your project can use libraries beyond the standard library.

items.prompt: What is an API in everyday programming terms?

choices.label: A defined interface for requesting and exchanging functionality or data.
A special kind of Python loop.

solution.summary: An API is an agreed-upon interface that lets one piece of software use data or behavior from another.

items.prompt: Why would you use a database instead of a plain text file for some applications?

solution.summary: Databases help when data needs structure, querying, indexing, relationships, or multi-user reliability.

items.prompt: Before adding a third-party package or API to a learning app, what should you check?

solution.summary: External tools accelerate development, but they also add operational and maintenance responsibilities.
checklist: Is the dependency trustworthy and maintained?
What data shape and failure modes should I expect?
How will I test the integration without breaking the core app?
